steps.title: Apply area formula and constraint
steps.content:
The area of triangle ABC with base $AC = 12$ and height $BC = y$ is:
$$\frac{1}{2} \cdot 12 \cdot y = 6y$$
The constraint states $48 \leq \text{Area} \leq 60$, so:
$$48 \leq 6y \leq 60$$
Dividing all parts by 6:
$$8 \leq y \leq 10$$
Since $y$ is an integer, we have $y \in \{8, 9, 10\}$.

steps.title: Express x in terms of y using the line equation
steps.content:
Line $AB$ connects $A(0, 0)$ to $B(12, y)$. Its equation is:
$$Y = \frac{y}{12}x$$
At the point where $x = 5$ (the distance from $A$ on the base), the point on line $AB$ has $y$-coordinate:
$$Y = \frac{y}{12} \cdot 5 = \frac{5y}{12}$$
The perpendicular from the base up to this point has height $x = \frac{5y}{12}$.

steps.title: Calculate x for each valid integer y
steps.content:
Using $x = \frac{5y}{12}$ with each possible integer value of $y$:
  • If $y = 8$: $x = \frac{5(8)}{12} = \frac{40}{12} = \frac{10}{3}$
  • If $y = 9$: $x = \frac{5(9)}{12} = \frac{45}{12} = \frac{15}{4}$
  • If $y = 10$: $x = \frac{5(10)}{12} = \frac{50}{12} = \frac{25}{6}$
Any one of these three values is a correct answer to the problem.

Equivalent forms accepted: either the fraction 10 over 3, the fraction 15 over 4, or the fraction 25 over 6.
